questions.title: Complete File Reading Code
questions.description: Drag tokens to complete file reading:
questions.explanation: File reading: fopen with "r" for read, check for NULL, fgets reads lines while != NULL, fclose closes file.
hints: Remember that check if fopen returned NULL
fgets returns NULL at end of file
Remember that always fclose the file
questions.hint: Consider proper file handling - always check if fopen() succeeded and close files when done.

questions.title: File Opening Modes
questions.description: Which mode appends to existing file?
questions.explanation: "a" mode opens for append. "w" truncates file. "r" reads only. "r+" reads and writes.
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: fgets vs scanf
questions.description: What's the difference?
options: fgets reads entire line including newline, scanf stops at whitespace
scanf is safer with buffer overflow
questions.explanation: Fgets reads entire line (including \n), scanf stops at first whitespace. fgets is safer (limit specified).
hints: Think about scanf stops at space/tab/newline
questions.hint: Think about: fgets reads entire line (including \n), scanf stops at first whitespace.

questions.title: EOF Detection
questions.description: How to detect end of file?
questions.explanation: Fgetc returns EOF (typically -1) when end of file is reached. Must compare with EOF macro.
hints: Remember that eOF is a macro, typically -1
questions.hint: Think about: fgetc returns eof (typically -1) when end of file is reached.

questions.title: fclose Return Value
questions.description: What should you do after using a file?
questions.explanation: Always close files with fclose(fp) to flush buffers and release resources.
questions.hint: Consider proper file handling - always check if fopen() succeeded and close files when done.

questions.title: fprintf to File
questions.description: What does this code do?
questions.explanation: Fprintf writes formatted output to a file. Mode "w" creates/overwrites the file.
questions.hint: Think about: fprintf writes formatted output to a file.

questions.title: fscanf Reading
questions.description: What does this program do?
options: Reads integer from file into num
questions.explanation: Fscanf reads formatted input from a file, like scanf but from file instead of stdin.
questions.hint: Think about: fscanf reads formatted input from a file, like scanf but from file instead of stdin.

questions.title: Read/Write Without Truncate
questions.description: Which mode opens a file for reading and writing without truncating it?
questions.explanation: "r+" opens for read/write and does not truncate. "w+" truncates.
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: w+ Behavior
questions.description: What happens when opening an existing file with "w+"?
options: File is opened for reading only
File is opened for append only
File is truncated to zero length
questions.explanation: "w+" truncates the file and opens it for read/write.
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: feof Timing
questions.description: When does feof(fp) become true?
options: After a read attempts to go past EOF
As soon as EOF is reached
questions.explanation: Feof becomes true only after a read fails due to EOF.
hints: Remember that eOF flag is set after failure
Remember that read past EOF triggers it
Remember that check read return value
questions.hint: Think about: feof becomes true only after a read fails due to eof.

questions.title: Seek to Start
questions.description: Which call moves the file position to the beginning?
questions.explanation: SEEK_SET with offset 0 moves to the start of the file.
questions.hint: Think about: seek_set with offset 0 moves to the start of the file.

questions.title: ftell Meaning
questions.description: What does ftell(fp) return?
questions.explanation: Ftell returns the current file offset in bytes.
questions.hint: Think about: ftell returns the current file offset in bytes.

questions.title: rewind Effect
questions.description: What does rewind(fp) do?
options: Moves position to start and clears errors
questions.explanation: Rewind sets the position to the start and clears error/EOF flags.
questions.hint: Think about: rewind sets the position to the start and clears error/eof flags.

questions.title: fread Return Value
questions.description: What does fread return?
questions.explanation: Fread returns the number of elements successfully read.
hints: Remember that returns count of elements
Remember that compare with requested count
questions.hint: Think about: fread returns the number of elements successfully read.

questions.title: Binary Read Mode
questions.description: Which mode opens a file for binary reading?
questions.explanation: "rb" opens a file for reading in binary mode.
questions.hint: Think about: "rb" opens a file for reading in binary mode.

questions.title: fopen Failure Check
questions.description: How do you check if fopen failed?
questions.explanation: Fopen returns NULL on failure.
hints: Think about eOF is for reads
questions.hint: Consider proper file handling - always check if fopen() succeeded and close files when done.

questions.title: perror Usage
questions.description: What does perror do after a failed fopen?
questions.explanation: Perror prints a message based on the current errno value.
questions.hint: Think about: perror prints a message based on the current errno value.

questions.title: fscanf Return
questions.description: What does fscanf return when reading integers?
options: EOF on end of file, otherwise number of items read
questions.explanation: Fscanf returns the count of successfully read items, or EOF on end of file.
questions.hint: Think about: fscanf returns the count of successfully read items, or eof on end of file.

questions.title: fgets Newline
questions.description: What does fgets do with the newline?
questions.explanation: Fgets keeps the newline if it is read before the buffer fills.
hints: Think about only if it fits
questions.hint: Think about: fgets keeps the newline if it is read before the buffer fills.

questions.title: Write a Char
questions.description: Which function writes a single character to a file?
questions.explanation: Fputc writes a single character to a file.
questions.hint: Think about: fputc writes a single character to a file.

questions.title: fprintf Target
questions.description: Where does fprintf write output?
options: The file pointed to by fp
questions.explanation: Fprintf writes formatted output to the given file stream.
hints: Remember that first argument is stream
Think about fp points to file
questions.hint: Think about: fprintf writes formatted output to the given file stream.

questions.title: Append Mode
questions.description: What is true about mode "a"?
options: Writes always go to the beginning
Writes always go to the end
questions.explanation: Append mode writes at the end of the file.
questions.hint: Think about: append mode writes at the end of the file.

questions.title: a+ Mode
questions.description: What does "a+" allow?
questions.explanation: "a+" allows reading and appending (writes go to end).
questions.hint: Think about: "a+" allows reading and appending (writes go to end).

questions.title: Remove File
questions.description: Which function deletes a file?
questions.explanation: Remove deletes a file in standard C.
questions.hint: Think about: remove deletes a file in standard c.

questions.title: Rename File
questions.description: Which function renames a file?
questions.explanation: Rename is the standard C function for renaming files.
hints: Remember that takes old and new names
questions.hint: Think about: rename is the standard c function for renaming files.

questions.title: fgetc Return Type
questions.description: Why is fgetc's return type int?
options: It must represent all char values plus EOF
questions.explanation: Int can represent all unsigned char values and EOF.
hints: Think about char may not hold EOF
questions.hint: Think about: int can represent all unsigned char values and eof.

questions.title: ungetc Purpose
questions.description: What does ungetc do?
options: Removes a character from the file
Pushes a character back to the stream
questions.explanation: Ungetc pushes a character back so it can be read again.
hints: Remember that next read returns same char
questions.hint: Think about: ungetc pushes a character back so it can be read again.

questions.title: Binary Struct Read
questions.description: What does this read?
questions.code_template:
struct Item x;
size_t n = fread(&x, sizeof(x), 1, fp);
questions.explanation: Fread reads 1 element of size sizeof(x) into x.
questions.hint: Think about what sizeof returns - the size in bytes, not the number of elements.

questions.title: rb+ Mode
questions.description: What does "rb+" mean?
questions.explanation: "rb+" opens for reading and writing in binary mode without truncating.
questions.hint: Think about: "rb+" opens for reading and writing in binary mode without truncating.

questions.title: Read Last Byte
questions.description: Which call moves to the last byte of a file?
questions.explanation: SEEK_END with offset -1 positions the stream at the last byte.
questions.hint: Think about: seek_end with offset -1 positions the stream at the last byte.

questions.title: Flush Output
questions.description: Which function flushes a stream's output buffer?
questions.explanation: Fflush forces buffered output to be written.
hints: Remember that used with output streams
questions.hint: Think about: fflush forces buffered output to be written.

questions.title: fclose Effect
questions.description: What does fclose do besides closing the file?
questions.explanation: Fclose flushes buffers and releases the file handle.
questions.hint: Consider proper file handling - always check if fopen() succeeded and close files when done.

questions.title: Safe Line Read
questions.description: Which is the safest way to read a line into buf?
questions.explanation: Fgets limits input by buffer size and is safer than gets/scanf.
hints: Consider using size to limit input
questions.hint: Think about: fgets limits input by buffer size and is safer than gets/scanf.

questions.title: Whitespace in fscanf
questions.description: What does fscanf with "%s" do?
options: Reads a full line including spaces
questions.explanation: %s stops at whitespace when reading strings. C strings are null-terminated character arrays. The null terminator '\0' marks the end of the string.
hints: Consider using fgets for full line
questions.hint: Think about: %s stops at whitespace when reading strings.

questions.title: Open Missing File
questions.description: What happens if you fopen with "r" on a missing file?
questions.explanation: "r" requires an existing file. fopen returns NULL if it doesn't exist.
hints: Consider r does not create files
questions.hint: Consider proper file handling - always check if fopen() succeeded and close files when done.

questions.title: Create New File
questions.description: Which mode creates a file if it doesn't exist?
questions.explanation: "w" creates the file if it doesn't exist (and truncates if it does).
questions.hint: Remember that when both operands are integers, C performs integer division which truncates the decimal part.

questions.title: r+ on Missing File
questions.description: What happens if you open a missing file with "r+"?
questions.explanation: "r+" requires an existing file and fails if missing.
hints: Consider r+ needs file to exist
questions.hint: Think about: "r+" requires an existing file and fails if missing.

questions.title: Append Creates File
questions.description: What happens if you open a missing file with "a"?
questions.explanation: Append mode creates the file if it doesn't exist.
hints: Remember that creates file if missing
questions.hint: Think about: append mode creates the file if it doesn't exist.

questions.title: Read Integers Loop
questions.description: Which loop correctly reads integers until EOF?
questions.explanation: Check fscanf return value to read until EOF.
questions.hint: Think about: check fscanf return value to read until eof.

questions.title: Write Line
questions.description: Which function writes a string to a file?
questions.explanation: Fputs writes a string to a file stream.
hints: Think about fputs is file version
questions.hint: Think about: fputs writes a string to a file stream.

questions.title: Binary Write
questions.description: How many bytes are written here?
questions.code_template:
int buf[4] = {1,2,3,4};
size_t n = fwrite(buf, sizeof(int), 4, fp);
questions.explanation: 4 elements * sizeof(int). With int=4 bytes, that's 16 bytes.
questions.hint: Think about what sizeof returns - the size in bytes, not the number of elements.

questions.title: Implementer fil skrivning
questions.description:
Skriv en funktion med signaturen `int write_numbers(const char *filename, int *arr, int n)` der skriver tallene i arrayet til en fil (et tal per linje).

Returnér 0 hvis succesfuld, -1 hvis filen ikke kan åbnes.

Brug fopen, fprintf og fclose.
hints: Remember that brug fopen(filename, "w") til at åbne filen for skrivning
Remember that check om fopen returnerede NULL
Remember that loop gennem arrayet og brug fprintf(file, "%d\n", arr[i])
Remember that husk at kalde fclose(file)
